summary: Small fighter common fixup PR that corrected several mismatches in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c and one struct field type in src/melee/ft/types.h so affected functions could reach 100% matching. The changes adjusted call formatting/order sensitivity, introduced an alternate inline helper to match absolute-value style codegen, replaced an incorrect x1A88 field access, used typed Fighter fields instead of M2C_FIELD, and changed Fighter_x1A88_t.x4 from u8 to s8 to support signed comparisons.
what_changed: In ftCo_800A0FB0, the mpLib_8004F008 argument list was reformatted/split so the two zero arguments are passed in the expected sequence for matching.

Added static inline inverseInlineE0(ftCo_Fighter* fp), which computes the absolute horizontal difference between fp->cur_pos.x and fp->x1A88.x54.x using a branch that returns either the negated difference or the original difference.
Changed ftCo_800A92CC to test inverseInlineE0(fp) > 60.0 instead of inlineE0(fp) > 60.0, implying the original inline helper had the wrong sign/branch behavior for the target assembly.
In ftCo_800AC30C, changed the signed check from fp->x1A88.x34 < 0 to fp->x1A88.x4 < 0.
In ftCo_800B3900, initialized Fighter* fp with GET_FIGHTER(gobj), added PAD_STACK(8), and removed the separate gobj->user_data assignment.
In ftCo_800B3900, replaced an M2C_FIELD increment at offset 0x1B04 with typed access fp->x1A88.x7C += 1.
In Fighter_x1A88_t, changed field x4 from u8 to s8, enabling signed comparisons against zero.
smart_moves: Introduced a second inline helper rather than forcing the existing inlineE0 helper to serve two subtly different codegen/sign cases.
Converted a raw M2C_FIELD access into a named struct field once the correct x1A88 member was identified, improving both readability and type evidence.
Fixed the header type for x1A88.x4 to signed s8 at the source of truth instead of relying on casts at each use site.
Used GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_800B3900, a common matching tactic when stack shape and object access patterns matter.
Kept the PR narrow: only two files changed and the body explicitly scoped it to small mismatches preventing 100% matching.
decomp_lessons: If a signed comparison against a byte field is needed for matching, verify the struct field signedness in the shared header; u8 vs s8 can directly affect generated code.
Replacing M2C_FIELD with typed struct access can be both a cleanup and a matching fix when the field offset is understood.
Similar-looking inline helpers may need separate variants when the target code uses a different sign or absolute-value pattern.
GET_FIGHTER(gobj) may produce different codegen from directly reading gobj->user_data; try the project macro when matching fighter callbacks.
PAD_STACK can be required for small residual mismatches even when the high-level logic is already correct.
Field-offset reasoning matters: the PR ties the old raw offset 0x1B04 to fp->x1A88.x7C and corrects another x1A88 field use from x34 to x4.
naming_conventions: Existing unidentified fighter-common functions retain address-style names such as ftCo_800A0FB0, ftCo_800A92CC, ftCo_800AC30C, and ftCo_800B3900.
Temporary inline helpers use descriptive but local names such as inlineE0 and inverseInlineE0.
Struct fields that are not yet semantically named remain offset-style names such as x1A88, x4, x7C, and x54.
Fighter object access follows project macro naming such as GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use helper inlines to steer branch and arithmetic codegen, especially for absolute-value-like float comparisons.
Adjust signedness in struct definitions to match signed load/compare behavior instead of patching locally.
Prefer known project macros such as GET_FIGHTER over equivalent-looking direct member access when exact assembly matters.
Use PAD_STACK to match stack frame layout in stubborn functions.
Remove M2C_FIELD once the corresponding typed struct field is known; typed access can change both clarity and generated code.
Small argument-list or expression-shape changes can affect matching even when semantics appear unchanged.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the intent: fix small mismatches in functions that prevented them from being 100% matched.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining M2C_FIELD accesses around Fighter x1A88 offsets that can be replaced with typed fields.
Audit other uses of Fighter_x1A88_t.x4 for signedness assumptions after the u8 to s8 change.
Look for other functions using inlineE0 where inverseInlineE0 or a similar absolute-difference helper might be required for matching.
Compare GET_FIGHTER(gobj) versus gobj->user_data in other fighter callbacks with stack or register mismatches.
